blocks.body: Present Simple vs Present Continuous — Present Simple = V1 / V-s for habits, facts, routines, and timetables, and Present Continuous = am / is / are + V-ing for actions happening now, around now, or temporarily.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"Present Simple usually answers what usually happens. Present Continuous usually answers what is happening now or around now.","rules":[{"label":"PRESENT SIMPLE","color":"blue","text":"V1 / V-s for habits, facts, routines, and timetables.","example":"She walks to school every day. / The train leaves at 7."},{"label":"PRESENT CONTINUOUS","color":"emerald","text":"am / is / are + V-ing for actions happening now, around the present moment, or temporarily.","example":"She is walking to school now. / I am staying with my aunt this week."},{"label":"KEY QUESTION","color":"violet","text":"Ask yourself: what usually happens? or what is happening now / around now?","example":"routine or fact / current action or temporary situation"}]}

{"title":"1. Quick comparison","intro":"Both tenses talk about the present, but the focus is different:","rows":[{"en":"Present Simple","ua":"a habit, fact, routine, or timetable","note":"He usually studies in the evening."},{"en":"Present Continuous","ua":"an action now, around now, or a temporary situation","note":"He is studying right now."},{"en":"every day / usually / often / always / sometimes / never","ua":"often point to Present Simple","note":"She always drinks tea in the morning."},{"en":"now / right now / at the moment / today / this week","ua":"often point to Present Continuous","note":"We are revising for the test this week."},{"en":"routines and timetables","ua":"often go with Present Simple","note":"The lesson starts at 9."},{"en":"temporary situations and current changes","ua":"often go with Present Continuous","note":"I am living with my cousin this month. / Prices are going up."},{"en":"stative verbs","ua":"usually stay in Present Simple, not in continuous","note":"I know the answer."}],"warning":"If you talk about a habit, fact, routine, or timetable, choose Present Simple. If you talk about an action now, around now, or something temporary, choose Present Continuous."}

{"title":"2. Forms grid","intro":"Compare the basic affirmative, negative, and question patterns.","items":[{"label":"Present Simple","title":"Affirmative","subtitle":"Subject + V1 / V-s: I work. / She works."},{"label":"Present Simple","title":"Negative","subtitle":"Subject + do not / does not + V1: They do not work. / He does not work."},{"label":"Present Simple","title":"Question","subtitle":"Do / Does + subject + V1? Do you work here? / Does she work here?"},{"label":"Present Continuous","title":"Affirmative","subtitle":"Subject + am / is / are + V-ing: I am working."},{"label":"Present Continuous","title":"Negative","subtitle":"Subject + am not / isn't / aren't + V-ing: He isn't working now."},{"label":"Present Continuous","title":"Question","subtitle":"Am / Is / Are + subject + V-ing? Are they working now?"}]}

{"title":"3. Where the focus changes the tense","sections":[{"label":"Habits, routines, and frequency","color":"blue","description":"Choose Present Simple for habits, routines, and frequency. Markers like every day, usually, often, always, sometimes, and never often point to this tense.","examples":[{"en":"I usually walk to work.","ua":"The important point is that this is a usual action."},{"en":"She never drinks coffee in the evening.","ua":"We talk about a habit, not an action now."}]},{"label":"Facts and timetables","color":"violet","description":"Choose Present Simple for facts, general truths, and timetables.","examples":[{"en":"Water boils at 100°C.","ua":"This is a general fact."},{"en":"The bus leaves at 8:15.","ua":"A timetable also often uses Present Simple."}]},{"label":"Now and at the moment","color":"emerald","description":"Choose Present Continuous for an action happening now, right now, or at the moment.","examples":[{"en":"I am talking to Anna right now.","ua":"The action is happening at the moment of speaking."},{"en":"They are doing their homework at the moment.","ua":"The focus is on what is happening now."}]},{"label":"Around now, temporary, with changes","color":"amber","description":"Choose Present Continuous for today, this week, temporary situations, and current changes.","examples":[{"en":"She is staying with friends this week.","ua":"This is a temporary situation, not a permanent fact."},{"en":"The weather is getting warmer.","ua":"We show a change happening now."}]},{"label":"A similar situation, a different focus","color":"teal","description":"The context can be similar, but the meaning changes: a habit or an action now.","examples":[{"en":"She works from home.","ua":"This is her usual routine."},{"en":"She is working from home today.","ua":"This is a temporary situation today."}],"note":"Present Simple often answers What usually happens?, and Present Continuous answers What is happening now / around now?"}]}

{"title":"4. Typical mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"blue","title":"Using Present Simple for an action happening now","wrong":"❌ I read the instructions now.","right":"✅ I am reading the instructions now."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"emerald","title":"Using Present Continuous for a habit or routine","wrong":"❌ She is usually taking the bus to school.","right":"✅ She usually takes the bus to school."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"violet","title":"Using continuous with stative verbs","wrong":"❌ I am knowing the answer.","right":"✅ I know the answer."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"amber","title":"Using Present Simple for a current change","wrong":"❌ Prices go up this month.","right":"✅ Prices are going up this month."}]}

{"title":"5. Stative verbs and an important note","items":["know, like, want, need, believe, and understand usually do not go in Present Continuous.","Say I know the answer or I want some water, not I am knowing the answer or I am wanting some water.","Some verbs can have a special meaning in continuous, but for A1–A2 keep the simple rule: state = usually Present Simple.","Choose Present Continuous for actions and changes, not for every verb with the marker now."]}

{"title":"6. A short decision guide","items":["Is it a habit, fact, routine, or timetable? → choose Present Simple.","Do you see every day, usually, often, always, sometimes, or never? → usually choose Present Simple.","Is it happening now, right now, or at the moment? → choose Present Continuous.","Is the situation temporary, happening today / this week, or changing now? → choose Present Continuous.","Is the verb stative, like know, like, want, or need? → usually stay with Present Simple."]}

{"title":"7. Pages and related topics in this block","items":[{"label":"Tenses","url":"/en/theory/tenses","current":false},{"label":"Present Simple","url":"/en/theory/present-simple","current":false},{"label":"Present Continuous","url":"/en/theory/present-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Present Simple vs Present Continuous","url":"/en/theory/tenses/present-simple-vs-present-continuous","current":true},{"label":"Present Perfect vs Present Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/tenses/present-perfect-vs-present-perfect-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Past Perfect vs Past Perfect Continuous","url":"/en/theory/tenses/past-perfect-vs-past-perfect-continuous","current":false}]}
